description: Labeled wallet dataset for scoring validation. Sybil labels are cited to a PUBLIC source (see label_source) — they attribute an existing community/project determination, not an original accusation. Labels are best-available ground truth, not certainty.

label_legend.likely_human: Behaves like a real, individual human operator (organic, long-lived, diverse activity).
label_legend.likely_sybil: Reported as a bot / airdrop-farming / Sybil address by a cited public source.

label_basis_legend.public_identity: Well-known public address; identity is widely documented on Etherscan and elsewhere.
label_basis_legend.community_sybil_report: Flagged in a public, methodology-documented Sybil report; label attributes that report.

known_limitations: The Sybil cluster below was flagged for sequential-transfer farming on Arbitrum (L2); our ingestion currently covers Ethereum mainnet only, so their mainnet features may under-represent the L2 Sybil pattern. See docs/scoring-eval.md.
The human class is small (2 verified public figures). The set is intentionally not padded with unverified addresses. Expanding verified humans is future work.
No verified 'borderline' category is included yet.

wallets.label: likely_human
wallets.reason: vitalik.eth — many years of organic activity, ENS identity, highly diverse counterparties and contract interactions.

wallets.label: likely_human
wallets.reason: A second well-known Vitalik Buterin address ('Vb') — long, organic, multi-year history.

wallets.label: likely_sybil
wallets.reason: Part of a 516-address sequential-transfer farming cluster on Arbitrum flagged in the Hop airdrop Sybil report #3.
